View on Instagram


paneer 🤝🏻 comfort food. The weather in Richmond has been so cloudy and moody lately, it inspired me (also got a new camera AHHH!!)

item.title: this is your sign to make sandwiches, hammock with friends and e...
item.description:
View on Instagram


this is your sign to make sandwiches, hammock with friends and enter a food coma by the end 😌Ingredients: -thinly sliced peppered turkey -havarti -sourdough bread -bacon-tomatoes -avocado -romain lettuce -red onion -@mailleus whole grain mustard-@dukes_mayonnaise light mayo -salt and pepper *assemble however you like!! We added everything listed above :) *drinks are @drinkpoppi *hammocks are from @thenorthface and @enohammocks *Maple Leaf Creme Cookies from @aldiusa*Outfit from @setactive

item.description:
View on Instagram


CRÈME BRÛLÉE!! A little different style OP with the new @gopro hero 13 black! 🥳 ngl… it was pretty awkward filming this in front of people HAHA BUT it was a blast and I couldn’t stop laughing

item.title: Just a fun little chicken stir fry 🕺🏻recipe below! Ingredients: ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Just a fun little chicken stir fry 🕺🏻recipe below! Ingredients: -1 chicken breast -1 servicing of rice noodles -small bunch of broccolini -3 green onions -3-4 garlic cloves (sliced) -2 eggs -soy sauce -limes-olive oil, salt and pepper Recipe: -prep everything: slice garlic, cut broccolini & green onions in half, cut chicken breast into thin slices (butterfly and slice!), whisk together 2 eggs, cook rice noodles according to package (usually add boiling water for 2-5 min depending on the type you get)-heat a large pan (or wok) on medium heat. Add oil and chicken, season with salt and pepper. Cook for about 2 min. add garlic, broccolini, and green onions cook for another 2 min. Add a couple tbsp of soy sauce. Mix and remove from the pan. -wipe pan, add oil and eggs. Quickly scramble and add noodles, toss and add chicken and veggies back. Toss to coat and serve!! I like adding more green onions and some lime to the top 🫶🏼 recipe from @gordongram (he has a YT video if that’s helpful but it seriously takes under 10 min and is so good!) *for those curious: the fisheye clips are filmed on @gopro !! I used the max lens mod, turned off max lens mode, and turned off hyper smooth (otherwise the circle will move around) mounted it with a magnetic clip above my stove 😌*other shots filmed on my iPhone13 switching between cinematic and normal video!

item.title: rain or shine we’re always down for a picnic 😤 (i wasn’t expecti...
item.description:
View on Instagram


rain or shine we’re always down for a picnic 😤 (i wasn’t expecting it to rain but we made the most of it hahah) recipe below !!! Ingredients: -sliced sourdough bread -sliced cracked pepper turkey breast (have the deli slice it for you, it’s so yum!) -sliced shallots-cherry tomatoes -mozzarella -pesto: 1cup basil, 1/3cup grated parm (I just added parm chunks because I’m lazy lol), 4 garlic cloves, 3tbsp olive oil, 1 1/2tbsp pine nuts, 2tbsp lemon juice, pinch of salt -mixed greens -balsamic glaze -olive oil, salt and pepper Recipe: -preheat oven to 450F (or set on high broil) -add pesto ingredients to a food processor. Blend until smooth and set aside. You’ll have extra! Store in fridge for 3-5 days. Use on toast, sandwiches, pasta.. etc :) -heat a pan on med heat. Add olive oil, tomatoes and shallots. Season w/ salt and pepper. Cook for 5ish min until shallots are slightly browned and tomatoes start to soften (I like to slightly squish them w a spatula). Remove from the pan -optional: add olive oil to bread and toast in the same pan as the tomatoes! -place turkey and mozzarella on one slice of bread. Add to oven and bake until the mozz is melted. -add pesto to the other slice, top with cooked tomatoes and shallots, mixed greens, drizzle with balsamic glaze. Close it up and enjoy !!! 🥳🫶🏼

item.title: Cold brew!!! For all my coffee lovers 😋 -1:16 ratio -best if it’...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Cold brew!!! For all my coffee lovers 😋 -1:16 ratio -best if it’s freshly ground (props to you if you have a grinder, I’m jealous. :’) If you don’t, ask your coffee shop to grind it and try to use it soon after!) -let sit for 24 hours! Some people do room temp, I prefer fridge. Up to you :) -for funsies: over extracted coffee tastes bitter and burnt. This can happen if it’s left in the water too long or the coffee is too fine. Under extracted is sour/tangy and watery. This can happen if it’s too coarse or didn’t sit long enough. Cold brew should be smooth and balanced without crazy bitterness/weird flavors. Have some fun dialing it in and make some fantastic coffee 😎 I’ve always said great CB doesn’t need cream ;) *I usually don’t talk about being a “coffee snob” because people tend to hate on it? With that being said I’ve just learned to appreciate great coffee and the process that goes into it. It’s one of the main things that got me into cooking and appreciating the art of food/cocktails/craft beer.
